events.title: Paul McCartney turns 84
events.blurb: Sir Paul McCartney, born on June 18, 1942, is an English singer, songwriter, and multi-instrumentalist who gained worldwide fame as the bassist and co-lead vocalist for the Beatles.

angles.name: Macca's Legacy
angles.copy: An absolute legend turns 84! What's your favourite Paul McCartney track, from The Beatles, Wings, or his solo career?

events.title: Alison Moyet turns 65
events.blurb: Alison Moyet, born on June 18, 1961, is an English singer, songwriter, and performer, known for her powerful bluesy voice and her work with the duo Yazoo before a successful solo career.

angles.name: Voice of an Era
angles.copy: Happy 65th birthday to the incredible Alison Moyet! Her distinctive voice has given us so many unforgettable songs. Which one is your go-to?

events.title: Lionel Richie turns 77
events.blurb: Lionel Richie, born on June 20, 1949, is an American singer, songwriter, composer, and record producer, known for his work with the Commodores and his hugely successful solo career.

angles.name: Hello, Lionel!
angles.copy: Seventy-seven years young! Happy birthday to the legendary Lionel Richie, whose timeless hits still get us singing along. What's your favourite?

events.title: Cyndi Lauper turns 73
events.blurb: Cyndi Lauper, born on June 22, 1953, is an American singer, songwriter, actress, and activist known for her distinctive voice and vibrant style.

angles.name: Girls Just Want Fun
angles.copy: Happy 73rd birthday to the one and only Cyndi Lauper! An icon of music and style, her songs are still absolute anthems. What's your favourite track?

events.title: Remembering George Michael, who would have been 63
events.blurb: George Michael, born Georgios Kyriacos Panayiotou on June 25, 1963, was an English singer-songwriter and record producer who died on December 25, 2016.

angles.name: A True Icon
angles.copy: Remembering the incredible talent of George Michael, who would have been 63 today. His music continues to inspire and move millions. What's your favourite song from him?

events.title: Anniversary of Michael Jackson's Death
events.blurb: Michael Jackson, born on August 29, 1958, was an American singer, songwriter, and dancer who died on June 25, 2009, at the age of 50.

angles.name: King of Pop Remembered
angles.copy: On this day, we remember the King of Pop, Michael Jackson, who passed away 17 years ago. His groundbreaking music and artistry continue to influence generations.

events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Richard Starkey, known professionally as Ringo Starr, born on July 7, 1940, is an English musician, singer, songwriter, and actor who achieved international fame as the drummer for the Beatles.

angles.name: Peace and Love, Ringo!
angles.copy: Happy 86th birthday to the legendary Ringo Starr! From The Beatles to his All-Starr Band, his rhythm is unmistakable. What's your favourite Ringo moment?

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Sir Brian Harold May, born on July 19, 1947, is an English musician, singer, songwriter, and astrophysicist, best known as the lead guitarist of the rock band Queen.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Happy 79th birthday to the incomparable Sir Brian May! His iconic guitar riffs have given us some of rock's greatest anthems. What's your favourite Queen track featuring his legendary sound?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Sir Michael Philip Jagger, born on July 26, 1943, is an English singer, songwriter, actor, and film producer, best known as the lead singer and one of the founding members of the Rolling Stones.

angles.name: Still Rocking
angles.copy: Can you believe Mick Jagger turns 83 today?! The ultimate frontman, still electrifying audiences worldwide. What's your top Rolling Stones track?

events.title: MTV Launched (1981)
events.blurb: MTV, the first 24-hour music video channel, launched on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed the Radio Star' as its first broadcast video.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star?
angles.copy: On this day in 1981, MTV launched and changed music forever! Remember staying up late to catch your favourite videos? What was the first video you saw on MTV?

events.title: Oasis released 'Definitely Maybe' (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis released their debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, which became an instant classic of the Britpop era.

angles.name: Britpop Classic
angles.copy: Thirty-two years since Oasis dropped their iconic debut, 'Definitely Maybe'! Which track from the album still gives you chills?

events.title: Woodstock Festival took place (1969)
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in music history, took place from August 15-18, 1969, in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Three Days of Peace & Music
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago today, the iconic Woodstock Festival began. A legendary gathering that defined a generation. If you could have been there, which artist would you have most wanted to see?

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's Death
events.blurb: Elvis Presley, born on January 8, 1935, the 'King of Rock and Roll,' died on August 16, 1977, at the age of 42.

angles.name: Long Live The King
angles.copy: Remembering Elvis Presley, who passed away 49 years ago today. His voice and charisma changed music forever. What's your favourite Elvis track?

events.title: Remembering Tupac Shakur, who would have been 55
events.blurb: Tupac Shakur, born on June 16, 1971, was an influential American rapper and actor who died on September 13, 1996, in Las Vegas, Nevada.

angles.name: A Voice Remembered
angles.copy: On this day, we remember the profound impact of Tupac Shakur, who would have been 55. His lyrics and legacy continue to resonate deeply.

events.title: Remembering Jimi Hendrix, who would have been 84
events.blurb: Jimi Hendrix, born on November 27, 1942, was an American guitarist, singer, and songwriter who died on September 18, 1970, at the age of 27.

angles.name: Guitar Legend Gone Too Soon
angles.copy: Remembering the unparalleled genius of Jimi Hendrix, who would have been 84. His innovative guitar work changed music forever. What's your favourite Hendrix track?

events.title: The Beatles released 'Abbey Road' (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles released their iconic album 'Abbey Road' on September 26, 1969, in the UK, featuring classic tracks like 'Something' and 'Come Together'.

angles.name: Crossing into History
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That iconic album cover and those timeless tracks. What's your go-to song from the album?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, born on September 7, 1951, is an American singer, songwriter, and musician, best known as the frontwoman of the rock band The Pretenders.

angles.name: Pretenders Icon
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! A true rock icon whose voice and attitude are unmistakable. What's your favourite Pretenders hit?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Liam Gallagher, born on September 21, 1972, is an English singer and songwriter, best known as the lead vocalist of the rock band Oasis and later Beady Eye, and for his successful solo career.

angles.name: Biblical!
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! The voice of a generation, still bringing the rock and roll. What's your favourite Liam track, Oasis or solo?

events.title: John Lennon released 'Imagine' (1971)
events.blurb: John Lennon released his iconic song 'Imagine' in October 1971, which became a global anthem for peace and unity.

angles.name: Imagine Peace
angles.copy: Fifty-five years since John Lennon gave us 'Imagine'. A powerful message that still resonates today. What does this song mean to you?

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the first-generation iPod on October 23, 2001, revolutionising portable music players and the way people listened to music.

angles.name: Pocket Full of Music
angles.copy: Twenty-five years ago, the first iPod changed everything! Remember loading up your entire music library onto one device? What was the first album you put on your iPod?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: James Bond Day is celebrated annually on October 5th, marking the anniversary of the world premiere of the first James Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Time to celebrate 007 and all his iconic adventures. Which Bond theme song is your absolute favourite?
